title: AgentRun interface — transport-agnostic lifecycle for agent capture and linking
description: Define the core AgentRun dataclass: bead_id, agent_id, prompt, trace_path, status, result. Implement ingest_agent_run() that: parses trace JSONL (regardless of source), creates a source linked to the bead, inserts thoughts/derivations, creates implemented_by edge. Same interface for subagent tool-results/ and docker cp output. This is the foundation all capture builds on.
close_reason: AgentRun interface implemented in tools/graph/agent_runs.py. parse_agent_trace() handles JSONL from both tool-results/ and /tmp/tasks/. ingest_agent_run() creates source, thoughts, derivations, entities, and cross-reference edges (spawned_by, implemented_by). discover_subagent_traces() finds traces in both locations. CLI: graph agent-runs [--list] [--session ID]. 87 subagent sessions ingested on first run.

title: Ingest subagent traces — tool-results/ JSONL files into graph
description: Claude Code stores subagent (sidechain) traces in tool-results/*.txt as JSONL, not in the main session JSONL. Our ingestion pipeline misses them entirely. Need to: discover tool-results/ dirs, parse each file as a subagent session, link to parent session, ingest thoughts/derivations. This is blocking all subagent observability — evals, research agents, code fix agents are all invisible.
close_reason: Covered by AgentRun interface — discover_subagent_traces() finds all tool-results/*.txt and /tmp/tasks/*.output files, filters to actual JSONL agent traces, ingests via the same pipeline. 87 traces ingested.

title: Fix graph search query escaping — FTS5 chokes on hyphens, special chars
description: FTS5 interprets hyphens as column operators: 'one-time' becomes 'column one minus time'. Queries with hyphens, colons, parentheses all break. Need to sanitize/quote user queries before passing to FTS5 MATCH. Also consider OR mode vs AND mode — current AND-only means 'scraper login auth session cookie' returns nothing because no single record has all 5 words. Should support both modes. This is blocking agent search efficiency right now.
close_reason: Fixed by Sonnet subagent in 56s. _sanitize_fts_query() wraps terms in quotes, strips operators, supports --or mode. Verified: hyphens no longer crash, multi-term OR queries work.

title: First autonomous bead execution — end-to-end proof of concept
description: Pick a real bead from the ready queue, dispatch it to a container, let it run to completion, collect results, verify bead was closed. The bootstrap moment.

title: Agent launch script — docker run with env injection and bead context
description: Shell script: takes a bead ID, runs bd show to get context, launches container with Claude auth token, GRAPH_SCOPE, bead description as the prompt. Uses --dangerously-skip-permissions. Mounts repo + databases.

title: Agent container image — Ubuntu + Claude Code + bd + graph + python
description: Dockerfile that bakes in: claude CLI, bd CLI, graph CLI, python3.12 + .venv with graph deps. Should start in <5s. No services, no DinD. Just a clean agent workspace.

title: Sprint 1: Eyes and Hands — visibility + first agent dispatch
description: Three parallel tracks: (A) Dashboard for operational visibility, (B) Foundation tooling so agents can use bd+graph, (C) Container dispatch so agents can run autonomously. Converges to: first bead executed by an autonomous agent, observable through the dashboard.

title: Dashboard skeleton — Starlette server, Tailwind, static file serving
description: Minimal Starlette app with uvicorn, Tailwind CDN, vanilla JS. Serves on localhost:8080. Hot reload for templates. Base layout with nav sidebar.
notes: Deprioritized: beads-ui covers bead board. Our dashboard skeleton should focus on what beads-ui DOESN'T have: graph explorer, session monitor, markdown viewer, annotations. Still needed but the scope narrowed.

title: Bead Board view — ready queue, dependency DAG, status lanes
description: Shows bd ready output, clickable beads with full detail, dependency graph visualization (simple DAG or tree), status columns (ready/in-progress/blocked/done). Calls bd ready --json, bd list --json, bd dep tree --json.
notes: beads-ui (community tool) running at http://100.122.70.30:3000 via tailnet. Renders all 32 beads, dependency tree, kanban view. Not mobile responsive. Accessible from phone. Consider this bead partially satisfied by beads-ui — our dashboard bead board can either wrap or replace it later.

title: Operational Dashboard — Web UI for beads, graph, sessions, agents
description: Starlette + Tailwind + vanilla JS dashboard. Bead board (ready queue, dependency DAG), session monitor (live agents, token burn), knowledge explorer (search, read, entities), playbook status, project overview, trace view. The operational visibility layer Jeremy has requested since session 1.

title: bd and graph CLIs permanently on PATH with agent permissions
description: Ensure bd and graph are in PATH for all sessions (bashrc). Add bd permission wildcards to settings.local.json. Verify both work from fresh shell.
close_reason: bd v0.60.0 and graph CLI both in PATH via ~/.local/bin wrappers. bd permissions added to settings.local.json. Verified from fresh shell.

title: Foundation: Beads + Graph Integration
description: Wire beads into the autonomy knowledge graph. bd commands available to agents via scoped wrappers. Session hooks capture bead state changes.

title: Agent Dispatch & Runtime
description: Spawn Claude Code and Codex instances from beads. Leverage GasTown agent registry patterns. tmux sessions with git worktree isolation. Support Claude Max subscription.

title: Autonomy Agentic Framework
description: Build the complete agentic development framework: beads-driven task tracking, agent dispatch, scoped graph access, result collection, and librarian automation. Bootstrap: use the system to build the system.

title: Bead-session edge linking — implemented_by, researched_by, informed_by relations
description: Extend graph edges to support bead↔session associations. Relations: implemented_by (bead→session that did the work), researched_by (bead→research session), informed_by (bead→source that provided context), spawned (parent session→subagent session). Enable graph queries like 'show all sessions for this bead' and 'what bead drove this session'.

title: Agent prompt composition — AGENTS.md + bead context + shared blocks
description: Build prompt composer: reads AGENTS.md shared blocks (tool guidelines, experience report, working style), combines with bd show output for a specific bead, adds GRAPH_SCOPE. Output is the complete prompt for an agent run. Used by both Agent tool dispatch and docker launch script.

title: Remote session observation — alternatives to /remote-control for monitoring agent sessions
description: Anthropic's /remote-control routes through their servers, too slow for permission approvals. Research: tmux attach for local, ttyd for web terminal, dashboard live-tail for polling, approval queue in dashboard UI. Short term: tmux. Medium term: dashboard. Long term: ACP (Agent Communication Protocol) from GasTown.

title: Markdown viewer — render graph content and session transcripts in browser
description: marked.js client-side rendering. Source reader view (graph read rendered as HTML). Session transcript viewer. Code syntax highlighting via highlight.js.

title: Session log review tooling — parse JSONL, compute tool use stats, flag issues
description: CLI command that analyzes a Claude Code session JSONL: tool call counts, search query success rate, repeated similar queries, time per tool, token efficiency. Feeds into prompt refinement.

title: Agent result collection — structured output from container to graph
description: When agent container exits: extract output files, parse structured result (completion status, tool feedback, discovered work), ingest into graph, update bead status via bd close/update.

title: Implement semantic vector search — sqlite-vec + nomic-embed + RRF hybrid
description: Research complete (musings/research-search-upgrade.md). Add sqlite-vec shadow table, embed 92K records with nomic-embed-text-v1.5 (384-dim Matryoshka), RRF fusion with FTS5, FlashRank reranker. Estimated: 2-3 days. Cuts agent query count 5-10x.

title: Knowledge Explorer — search, source reader, entity browser
description: Search box that calls graph search. Results link to full source reader (graph read). Entity browser showing mention counts and cross-references. Project filter dropdown.

title: Session Monitor — live and recent agent sessions
description: Shows active and recent Claude Code sessions from graph. Token consumption, wall time, turn count. For live sessions (JSONL still growing), show latest turn with auto-refresh. Calls graph sources --type session --project X.

title: Bridge: graph CLI can query bead status, bd can reference graph sources
description: graph beads command that calls bd ready/list/show. Lets agents use one tool to see both knowledge and work items. Could also mean ingesting bead state into the graph for search.
notes: Scope expanded: not just CLI bridge but the full association layer. Edges between beads (Dolt) and graph sources (SQLite). Relations: implemented_by, researched_by, informed_by, spawned. Critical for audit trail and discovery. See session discussion on bead-session linking.

title: Scoped bd wrappers — agents get project-locked bead access
description: Like graph-scope for GRAPH_SCOPE, create bd wrappers that lock agents to specific bead prefixes or databases. Agent runs bd ready and only sees beads in its scope.

title: bd prime integration — inject graph context alongside bead context
description: bd prime gives agents bead workflow context. We also need graph context (available commands, current scope, project stats). Either extend bd prime or create a complementary graph prime command that agents get at session start.

title: Agent instructions file — AGENTS.md with bd+graph workflow
description: Write AGENTS.md that tells agents: use bd ready to find work, bd show for details, graph search/read for context, bd update for progress, bd close when done. The complete agent onboarding doc.

title: Focus: Abstract Work Orchestrator
description: The meta-layer that manages all autonomous work. Priority queue, environment registry, dispatch strategies, monitoring, tracing, cost tracking. Sits above beads and agent dispatch.

title: Non-Code Result Collection
description: GasTown collects results as git commits. We need result collection for research, playbooks, graph updates, and other non-code outputs. Structured output format, validation, graph ingestion.

title: Linux Agent Isolation
description: Solve the container/sandbox gap on Linux. GasTown uses macOS sandbox-exec. We need DinD, namespaces, seccomp, or E2B for equivalent isolation. Evaluate cost/complexity tradeoffs.

title: Experience report parsing — extract structured feedback from agent output
description: Parser for experience_report.md (design in musings/design-agent-observability.md). Extracts: completion status, confidence, tool experience, failed queries, discovered beads, prompt issues. Feeds into analytics pipeline. Part of the AgentRun result processing.

title: Annotation interface — comment on paragraphs in past sessions
description: Paragraph-level annotations on session content. Click a paragraph, add a comment. Stored in graph as annotation source type. Cross-session references.

title: Unified secret management — encrypted store, container injection, rotation tracking
description: Research complete (musings/research-secret-management.md). Covers Claude auth, Anthropic API key, GitHub tokens, Slack tokens, mesh certs. Phase 1: docker env vars. Phase 2: infra-style asymmetric encryption with single master password. Phase 3: focus secrets inject.

title: Trace View — bead to dispatch to session to result chain
description: Given a bead ID, show its full lifecycle: creation, dispatch, agent session, result. Links bead metadata to session source in graph. Requires bead-graph bridge (auto-7o3) to be complete.

title: Session hooks — auto-sync beads on session start and pre-compact
description: SessionStart hook runs bd prime + graph sessions. PreCompact hook captures bead state. Ensures continuity across context compaction and new sessions.

title: Ingest bead state into knowledge graph for cross-reference
description: Periodically or on-change, export bd list --json and ingest into graph as a beads source type. Enables graph search to find related beads, and beads to reference graph sources.

title: Autonomy Model Alignment
description: Orient the framework around the Autonomy sovereignty model. Self-hosted, user-sovereign, scoped access. Mesh networking, internal DNS, secure agent environments. GRAPH_SCOPE enforcement.

title: Librarian Agents
description: Continuously running agents that maintain knowledge quality. Goal tracker (sessions→beads), playbook librarian (sessions→playbooks), spec validator (specs↔implementations), session annotator.
